violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must register in, and successfully complete, an approved course in food safety management. An employee must then register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. List of approved courses and application for Certified Food Safety Manager provided. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Dishwasher located in the Admiral Pub is not in good repair. It is not currently being used. 3-bay sink is being used to wash, rinse, and sanitize all food equipment. PIC at the Admiral Pub is working with repair service to have the dishwasher fixed. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2025
The ceiling of the Admiral Pub walk-in cooler is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must register in, and successfully complete, an approved course in food safety management. One employee must then register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils in Admiral Pub were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service call placed. 3-bay sink must be used to sanitize until dishwasher is reaching proper sanitizing temperature. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Per interview, staff does not sanitize barware (Admiral Pub) after washing and rinsing. After being cleaned, barware must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher in both Tav Vino and Admiral Pub. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sinks. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2025
The ceiling of the Admiral Pub walk-in cooler is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2025
